models.generation_context: 4th-gen (BP), introduced for 2019 MY; mid-cycle refreshes through 2024. 2026 reorganizes packaging across S Select Sport / Preferred / Carbon Edition / Turbo Premium Plus.
models.model_summary: Mazda's compact sedan, positioned more upscale than mainstream rivals with a refined chassis and high-quality interior. The base 2.5L NA engine (186 hp) powers FWD trims; the Carbon Edition adds AWD as standard, and the 2.5 Turbo Premium Plus (250 hp on premium fuel) is AWD-only. All trims use a 6-speed automatic; the hatchback-exclusive Premium adds a 6-speed manual.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet published a VDS score specific to the 2026 Mazda3 (VDS measures 3-year-old vehicles). Mazda as a brand has finished consistently in the top tier of recent Consumer Reports brand reliability rankings, and the current-gen Mazda3 (on sale since 2019) has a generally strong historical record.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a model-specific 2026 APEAL score. Owner surveys on KBB and Edmunds skew positive, with 74% of KBB respondents giving 5/5 stars.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Mazda3's premium interior, composed handling, and standard 5-star NHTSA / IIHS TSP+ safety package, with the optional 250-hp turbo and standard AWD on uplevel trims giving it character competitors lack. Common criticisms are tight rear seat and trunk room, a non-touch base infotainment system at lower speeds, and a top-trim price that overlaps entry-luxury sedans.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the premium-feeling interior, build quality, ride refinement, and AWD availability. Common complaints involve back-seat tightness, limited trunk volume for the class, and infotainment quirks (touchscreen disabled while in motion on non-Premium trims).

trims.notes: Base trim of the Mazda3 Sedan FWD/NA powertrain line. Touchscreen functionality on the 8.8-inch display is restricted while the car is moving on non-Premium trims (knob-only); Premium and Premium Plus trims add touch support. 0-60 is an estimate based on published Edmunds/U.S. News figures (~7.5-7.7 sec) since Mazda does not publish 0-60 for this configuration. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-mazda3-sedan); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

notable_other: Leather-wrapped steering wheel and shift knob

changes: Mazda Advanced Keyless Entry, rain-sensing wipers, dual-zone climate added
Leather-wrapped steering wheel and shift knob

trims.notes: Shares photography with other FWD S-line trims.

notable_other: 8-way power driver seat with lumbar and memory

changes: 8-way power-adjustable driver's seat with lumbar and memory
Includes Select Sport content (18-inch wheels, keyless entry, dual-zone climate, leatherette)

notable_other: Red leather interior with three select exterior colors

Distinctive red leather interior with limited exterior color palette
Wireless Apple CarPlay/Android Auto and Qi wireless charging added

EPA combined MPG drops to 29 (from 30 FWD) due to AWD

notable_other: Skyactiv-G 2.5 Turbo (250 hp / 320 lb-ft on 93 octane; 227 hp / 310 lb-ft on 87)

trims.notes: Base trim of the Mazda3 Sedan turbocharged AWD powertrain line (singleton: sole turbo trim). Output is 250 hp / 320 lb-ft on 93-octane premium; drops to 227 hp / 310 lb-ft on 87-octane regular per Mazda. 0-60 figure from Car and Driver testing. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-mazda3-sedan); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

models.notes: Mazda3 Sedan groups into two powertrain lines: NA 2.5L (S, Select Sport, Preferred FWD; Carbon Edition AWD) and 2.5 Turbo (Premium Plus AWD only). Step-up content beyond the base S trim is sourced from Mazda's 2026 packaging press release; manufacturer compare-trims page would not yield a granular spec table on fetch. Image entries for most trims point to mazdausa.com pages with needs_scraping: true since direct CDN asset URLs were not surfaced for individual trim galleries; the one verified press-release image is on the base S.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen (BP), introduced for 2019 MY. Shares platform/interior with Mazda3 Sedan; only body the 6-speed manual is offered on (Premium trim).
models.model_summary: Mazda's compact hatchback variant of the Mazda3, with a more rakish roofline and slightly more cargo room. Same powertrain lineup as the sedan (186-hp 2.5L NA or 250-hp 2.5 Turbo on premium fuel) plus a hatchback-exclusive 2.5 S Premium trim that pairs the NA engine with a 6-speed manual transmission.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet published a VDS score specific to the 2026 Mazda3 Hatchback. Mazda's recent Consumer Reports brand-reliability ranking has been top-tier, and the current-generation Mazda3 platform has a generally strong historical record.

customer_satisfaction.summary: No model-specific 2026 APEAL score published. Owner feedback skews positive on KBB and Edmunds.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Mazda3 Hatchback's premium feel, sharp handling, and the only-here 6-speed manual on the Premium trim; the turbo Premium Plus is among the quickest non-luxury compacts. Common drawbacks are limited rear visibility from the wide C-pillar, smaller cargo than mainstream-brand hatch rivals (Civic Hatch, Corolla Hatch), and high pricing on uplevel trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the premium-feel interior, refined ride, AWD availability, and the rare offering of a manual transmission. Common complaints include rear visibility from the thick C-pillars, modest cargo, and high-trim pricing close to entry-luxury territory.

trims.notes: Base trim of the Mazda3 Hatchback NA FWD powertrain line. EPA fuel economy is slightly lower than the sedan (26/34/29 vs 27/36/30) due to aerodynamic differences. Cargo volume measurement uses Mazda's published 20.1/47.1 cuft (behind 2nd row / max). 0-60 estimate based on published reviews. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-mazda3-hatchback); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

changes: Rain-sensing wipers and dual-zone climate added

notable_other: 8-way power driver seat with lumbar and memory

changes: 8-way power driver seat with lumbar and memory
Includes Select Sport content (18-inch wheels, keyless entry, dual-zone climate, leatherette)

notable_other: Mazda Online Navigation with OTA updates

trims.notes: Base trim of the Mazda3 Hatchback NA + 6-speed manual powertrain line (singleton: sole manual-transmission trim in the entire Mazda3 lineup). EPA rates the manual at 25/34/28; Mazda treats this as its own marketing trim. Premium-grade features (Bose, leather, HUD, OTA nav) come standard. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-mazda3-hatchback); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

notable_other: Red leather interior with three select exterior colors

trims.notes: Base trim of the Mazda3 Hatchback turbocharged AWD powertrain line (singleton). Curb weight estimated from the sedan turbo (3,417 lb) plus hatchback body penalty; Mazda's published spec deck would confirm. 0-60 in 5.6 sec from C&D testing. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-mazda3-hatchback); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

models.notes: Three powertrain lines on the Mazda3 Hatchback: NA + automatic FWD (S, Select Sport, Preferred), NA + automatic AWD (Carbon Edition), and a singleton NA + 6-speed manual FWD (S Premium); plus the singleton 2.5 Turbo Premium Plus AWD line. Each powertrain singleton is is_base_trim:true / delta_from_base:null per the sole-trim rule.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen (DM), introduced for 2020 MY; sits on the Mazda3 platform. 2026 adds Aire Edition trims and applies a mid-year MSRP adjustment effective May 4, 2026.
models.model_summary: Mazda's subcompact crossover, sharing platform and powertrain with the Mazda3 but with raised ride height, more cargo, and standard i-Activ AWD across the lineup. 186-hp 2.5L NA engine on most trims; a 2.5 Turbo (250 hp on premium) is available on Aire Edition and Premium Plus.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet published a model-specific VDS score for the 2026 CX-30. The current generation has historically scored at or above segment average on Consumer Reports.

customer_satisfaction.summary: No 2026 APEAL score published; owner feedback generally positive.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the CX-30's upscale interior, composed handling, and standard AWD at a competitive price; the optional 2.5 Turbo gives it sports-sedan-like acceleration. Common criticisms are tight rear seat and cargo for the segment, and a busy infotainment that uses a knob rather than touch on lower trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews not yet aggregated in sufficient volume for 2026 MY at the time of research.

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-30 NA powertrain line. MSRP reflects May 4, 2026 adjustment ($26,375); prior Sept 2025 announcement listed $26,415. Dimensions cross-checked from dealer site; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch. 0-60 estimated; Mazda does not publish a 0-60 spec for the NA CX-30.

White leatherette with gray suede-like trim

changes: NEW for 2026: blackout exterior badging package
White leatherette interior with gray suede-like trim
Includes Preferred-grade content (10.25-inch screen, moonroof, wireless charging, power driver seat)

notable_other: 2.5 Turbo (250 hp / 320 lb-ft on 93 octane; 227/310 on 87)

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-30 2.5 Turbo powertrain line. Output is 250/320 on 93 octane; 227 hp/310 lb-ft on 87. Turbo Aire is the lower of two turbo trims but has its own appearance package; treated here as a standalone trim_family. Curb weight estimated from turbo CX-30 figures. Dimensions cross-checked from dealer site; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch.

notable_other: Level-2 driver assistance (Cruising and Traffic Support)

changes: Adds Cruising and Traffic Support (Level-2 hands-on assist)
Removes Aire blackout package; standard chrome accents

trims.notes: Step-up over the Turbo Aire Edition in the same powertrain line.

models.notes: Two powertrain lines: 2.5L NA (S, Select Sport, Preferred, Aire Edition, Carbon Edition, S Premium) and 2.5 Turbo (Turbo Aire Edition, Turbo Premium Plus). Within the turbo line, Turbo Aire Edition is the lower-MSRP base; Premium Plus references it as from_trim. NHTSA/IIHS ratings inherited from Mazda's brand TSP+ award announcement.

models.generation_context: All-new 3rd generation, introduced for 2026 MY. Larger than the outgoing 2nd-gen; built on Mazda's small-platform architecture. Hybrid variant launches for the 2027 MY.
models.model_summary: Mazda's best-selling US vehicle, fully redesigned for 2026 with a longer wheelbase, larger cargo area, and Google built-in infotainment with Gemini AI. Single powertrain at launch: 187-hp 2.5L NA I4 with 6-speed automatic and standard i-Activ AWD on all trims.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS (Feb 2026) did not list the CX-5 among model-level award winners; Mazda as a brand sits mid-pack on JD Power VDS. Consumer Reports 2026 Annual Auto Reliability Survey put Mazda at a reliability score of 55 (#6 brand) — the brand dropped eight spots in 2026 largely due to the redesigned CX-70 and CX-90; the outgoing 2nd-gen CX-5 had been a top performer with predicted reliability typically 4-5/5. The brand-new 3rd-generation CX-5 (on sale less than a MY) has no model-specific CR predicted reliability yet; CR rates new generations conservatively until field data accumulates.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of May 2026 (APEAL typically publishes in July). The prior-gen CX-5 historically scored above segment average on APEAL. Will be revisited when 2026 APEAL is released.

professional_reviews.summary: Early reviews praise the redesigned 3rd-gen CX-5's larger and quieter cabin, refined ride, and the new Google built-in infotainment, but several reviewers note that dropping the 2.5 Turbo at launch removes the spicy character of the outgoing model and that competitors (RAV4, CR-V) still beat it on rear-seat and cargo space.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews not yet aggregated in significant volume for the brand-new 2026 redesign.

trims.notes: Base trim of the all-new 3rd-gen CX-5 (single powertrain line at launch). NHTSA and IIHS have not yet rated the redesigned model; left null with confidence accordingly. 0-60 estimate based on similar Mazda 187-hp AWD applications. Dimensions cross-checked from dealer site; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch.

changes: Tinted privacy glass + heated auto-folding mirrors
Heated front seats and heated steering wheel
Ventilated front seats and heated rear seats

notable_other: Cruising and Traffic Support with Lane Change Assist

changes: Driver Personalization System with face recognition

trims.notes: Top trim; level-2 driver assist standard.

models.notes: All-new 3rd-gen CX-5. Single 2.5L NA powertrain at launch with i-Activ AWD standard. Hybrid arrives for 2027 MY. NHTSA/IIHS ratings null because the redesigned vehicle had not yet been tested at time of research.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen, introduced for 2023 MY; built in Huntsville, AL at Mazda Toyota Manufacturing. Outdoor-focused 2-row CUV sibling to the CX-5. 2026 adds Meridian Edition trims and a white-interior option.
models.model_summary: Mazda's more outdoorsy 2-row crossover, with longer wheelbase and lower roof than the CX-5. Two gas powertrain lines: 187-hp 2.5L NA (S Select / S Preferred / S Meridian / S Premium) and 256-hp 2.5 Turbo (Turbo / Turbo Meridian / Turbo Premium Plus). Hybrid variants are catalogued separately as 'CX-50 Hybrid'.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet published a 2026-specific VDS score. The 1st-gen CX-50 has been on sale since 2023 and has earned average-to-above-average reliability marks; long-term data is still maturing.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers like the CX-50's premium interior, sharp on-road dynamics, and rugged styling, with the 2.5 Turbo providing strong acceleration. Cargo space and ground clearance are competitive but rear-seat headroom is tighter than the CX-5 due to the lower roofline.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates skew positive for the model line; specific 2026 MY sample sizes not yet reported.

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-50 NA powertrain line. EPA 26 combined; 24/30 city/hwy. NHTSA 5-star rating carried over from 2025 MY (same model). Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-50); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

Includes Preferred-level content (moonroof, heated seats, power driver)

trims.notes: New for 2026: Meridian Edition is now offered on the NA powertrain in addition to Turbo.

notable_other: 20-inch black metallic alloys (or silver with white-interior option)
Skyactiv-G 2.5 Turbo (256 hp / 320 lb-ft on 93 octane; 227/310 on 87)

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-50 2.5 Turbo powertrain line. 256 hp / 320 lb-ft on 93 octane; 227/310 on 87. Towing capacity 3,500 lb. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-50); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

notable_other: NEW for 2026: Turbo Meridian outdoor-themed package

changes: NEW 2026: Meridian Edition outdoor package on Turbo
Gloss-black Mazda emblems and badges (new for 2026)

models.notes: Two gas powertrain lines covered here: NA (S Select base) and Turbo (Turbo base). CX-50 Hybrid trims are catalogued as a separate model entry per Mazda's marketing (dedicated mazdausa.com/vehicles/cx-50-hybrid page). NHTSA rating carried from 2025 MY (same model).

models.generation_context: Hybrid variant of the CX-50, introduced for 2025 MY. Uses Toyota-derived hybrid system (THS) producing 219 hp combined; built alongside the gas CX-50 in Huntsville, AL. Mazda markets it on its own dedicated page.
models.model_summary: Toyota-derived hybrid version of the CX-50, pairing a 2.5L NA Atkinson-cycle engine with three electric motors and an eCVT, delivering 219 hp combined and 38 combined MPG. Standard electronic AWD via rear-axle motor on all trims.

reliability.summary: Hybrid system is shared with the Toyota RAV4 Hybrid, which has a strong long-term reliability record. JD Power has not yet published a 2026-specific VDS for CX-50 Hybrid.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the CX-50 Hybrid for its 38-MPG combined efficiency and the proven Toyota hybrid powertrain, paired with Mazda's premium interior. The trade-offs versus the gas turbo CX-50 are reduced peak power and a less Mazda-like driving feel from the eCVT.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 MY owner reviews aggregated as of research date.

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-50 Hybrid (sole powertrain line; single trim family per powertrain). Mazda does not publish battery kWh capacity for the Toyota-supplied THS pack; left null. Total range ~530 mi computed from 14.0 gal × 38 MPG plus modest hybrid reserve. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-50-hybrid); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

changes: Red leather (option) or black leather replaces leatherette
Includes Hybrid Premium content (ventilated leather seats, Bose, HUD)

models.notes: CX-50 Hybrid catalogued as a distinct model per Mazda's separate marketing page. Single hybrid powertrain across three trims. Battery kWh capacity not published by Mazda.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen, introduced for 2024 MY as the 2-row sibling to the 3-row CX-90. Shares Mazda's Large Architecture, longitudinally-mounted inline-6 with 48V M-Hybrid Boost, and 8-speed automatic.
models.model_summary: Mazda's 2-row midsize SUV, sharing its 199-inch length and 122-inch wheelbase with the 3-row CX-90 but seating only five with more cargo. 3.3L turbocharged inline-6 with 48V mild hybrid in two outputs (280 hp regular fuel or 340 hp premium), 8-speed automatic, standard i-Activ AWD.

reliability.summary: CX-70 has been on sale less than two model years; long-term VDS data not available. Consumer Reports' 2024 launch coverage flagged early powertrain calibration concerns on the related CX-90, which Mazda has addressed via software updates.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the CX-70's premium interior, ample cargo, and inline-6 powertrain refinement, but several call out that it offers little practical advantage over the 3-row CX-90 at similar price points and that ride quality on 21-inch wheels is firmer than rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews limited; the model line is still new.

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-70 3.3 Turbo MHEV powertrain line. NHTSA has not separately tested CX-70 from CX-90; left null. ev_battery_yr_mi is for the 48V mild-hybrid battery. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-70); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

changes: Towing mode for Mi-Drive enables full 5,000-lb tow rating
Nappa leather (black or red) replaces synthetic leather

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-70 high-output 3.3 Turbo S powertrain line (340 hp, premium fuel required). Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-70); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

changes: Top trim of the high-output line

models.notes: Two powertrain lines on the gas/MHEV CX-70: standard-output 3.3 Turbo (regular fuel) and high-output 3.3 Turbo S (premium fuel). CX-70 PHEV is catalogued as a separate model entry. Effective May 4, 2026, base CX-70 pricing increased; numbers here reflect the post-May-4 levels per the Oct 7, 2025 packaging release with subsequent May adjustment.

models.generation_context: PHEV variant of the CX-70, introduced for 2026 MY (new for the model line; previously only on CX-90). Uses Mazda's e-Skyactiv PHEV powertrain: 2.5L NA I4 + electric motor through an 8-speed automatic, with a 17.8 kWh battery.
models.model_summary: Plug-in hybrid version of the CX-70, combining a 2.5L NA I4 with an electric motor through an 8-speed automatic for 323 hp combined, ~32 miles of EPA electric range and ~520 miles total range. Standard i-Activ AWD; 5-seat 2-row layout.

reliability.summary: New for the CX-70 line in 2026; powertrain is shared with the CX-90 PHEV which has been on sale since 2024 with mixed early reliability feedback. Long-term VDS data not yet available.

professional_reviews.summary: Early reviewers note the CX-70 PHEV offers an attractive electric range and combined output for the segment, with the same premium interior as the gas CX-70. The 8-speed automatic plus PHEV system has occasional low-speed driveline lurch that Mazda has continued to refine via software updates.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner reviews not yet aggregated in significant volume.

notable_other: e-Skyactiv PHEV powertrain: 2.5L I4 + electric motor

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-70 PHEV powertrain line. EPA published 61 MPGe combined on the CX-70 PHEV per fueleconomy.gov (with electric range / total range not displayed on the model browse page); manufacturer cites 26-mile electric range and ~490 mi total. Specific MPGe value on individual fueleconomy.gov page would refine. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-70-phev); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

models.notes: Single PHEV powertrain line (e-Skyactiv) across two trims. EPA combined MPG / electric range published on individual fueleconomy.gov entry; model browse page does not display electric range. Battery kWh from Mazda technical specs.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen, introduced for 2024 MY as Mazda's flagship 3-row SUV. Shares Large Architecture and inline-6 MHEV powertrains with the 2-row CX-70. 2026 adds a Turbo S Premium Sport blackout trim and standard Alexa.
models.model_summary: Mazda's flagship 3-row SUV with longitudinal inline-6 turbocharged powertrain and 48V mild hybrid. Two powertrain outputs (280 hp standard / 340 hp on premium fuel), 8-speed automatic, standard i-Activ AWD, 7- or 8-passenger seating depending on trim.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports has rated CX-90 predicted reliability below average since launch, with early reports of transmission/driveline calibration issues that Mazda has continued to address via software updates. JD Power VDS not yet published for 2026.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the CX-90's upscale interior, smooth inline-6, and competitive third-row 3-row package, but also note driveline harshness at low speeds (an ongoing Mazda software focus) and a firmer-than-rivals ride on 21-inch wheels.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise interior quality, power, and third-row usability; common complaints involve low-speed driveline behavior and Mazda Connect quirks.

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-90 3.3 Turbo MHEV powertrain line. NHTSA 5-star carried from 2024 MY (same model). Standard 8-passenger seating; 7-passenger captain's chairs are optional on higher trims. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-90); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

changes: Heated front seats and steering wheel
Leather seats with ventilated front (vs cloth)

trims.notes: 7- or 8-passenger configuration; price shown is for 8-seat bench.

notable_other: NEW for 2026: blackout Premium Sport exterior treatment
Black chrome Signature Wing and Inline 6 badging

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-90 3.3 Turbo S (high-output) powertrain line. NEW for 2026: Premium Sport blackout exterior treatment exclusive to this trim. 340 hp requires premium fuel. Source citation for dimensions updated from cars.com to canonical manufacturer page (https://www.mazdausa.com/vehicles/2026-cx-90); values cross-validated against cars.com listing during research.

notable_other: Top trim of the CX-90 gas/MHEV lineup

changes: Top trim of the high-output line

models.notes: Two gas/MHEV powertrain lines on the CX-90: standard 3.3 Turbo (regular fuel) and 3.3 Turbo S (premium fuel). PHEV is catalogued separately. NHTSA 5-star rating carried from 2024 MY (same model). Pricing reflects post-May-4-2026 levels.

models.generation_context: PHEV variant of the CX-90, introduced at the model's 2024 launch. Uses Mazda's e-Skyactiv PHEV powertrain: 2.5L NA I4 + electric motor through an 8-speed automatic; 17.8 kWh battery.
models.model_summary: Mazda's plug-in hybrid 3-row SUV. 323 hp combined output, ~27-mile EPA electric range, ~500-mile total range. 7-passenger seating, standard i-Activ AWD via 8-speed automatic.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports has rated CX-90 (including PHEV) predicted reliability below average since launch. Mazda continues to address early calibration issues via software updates.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the CX-90 PHEV gives the model line strong combined output and useful electric-only range with the same premium 3-row interior. Drawbacks: lower towing (3,500 lb vs 5,000 on gas) and the same low-speed driveline behavior as the gas CX-90.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 MY owner reviews aggregated as of research date.

trims.notes: Base trim of the CX-90 PHEV powertrain line. EPA fueleconomy.gov shows 56 MPGe combined; gas-only mode 26 MPG combined. Mazda cites ~26-27 mile electric range and 490-500 mi total. 7-passenger captain's chairs standard. Dimensions cross-checked from secondary source; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch.

models.notes: Single PHEV powertrain line across three trims. CX-90 PHEV catalogued as a separate model per Mazda's dedicated marketing page. EPA combined MPGe 56 from fueleconomy.gov; manufacturer cites 26-mile electric range.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen ND, introduced for 2016 MY; refreshed for 2024 with the Skyactiv-G 2.0L upgraded to 181 hp and updated electronics. Soft-top body style; RF retractable-hardtop is catalogued as a separate model.
models.model_summary: Mazda's iconic lightweight rear-drive 2-seat soft-top roadster. Skyactiv-G 2.0L NA I4 making 181 hp / 151 lb-ft, paired with a 6-speed manual or 6-speed automatic. Curb weight under 2,400 lb on manual trims. Three trims: Sport, Club (manual-only), and Grand Touring (manual or auto).

reliability.summary: MX-5 historically rates above average for reliability on Consumer Reports owner surveys; the ND4 generation has been on sale since 2016 with a mature parts and service history.

customer_satisfaction.summary: No 2026 APEAL score published; MX-5 historically scores at or near top of sports-car segment in past APEAL studies.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to praise the MX-5 for its lightweight chassis, communicative steering, and joyful manual transmission, with strong value compared to other sports cars. Persistent criticisms: limited trunk space, no real luggage room, and a cabin that's tight for taller drivers; performance is brisk rather than fast at 6.5-second 0-60.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners consistently rate the MX-5 highly for driving fun and reliability; common complaints involve cabin storage and road noise from the soft top.

trims.notes: Base trim of the MX-5 Miata (soft top) manual powertrain line. NHTSA does not crash-test the MX-5; IIHS likewise does not rate the soft-top MX-5 (low-volume convertible). MSRP $30,430 from the Jan 27, 2026 packaging release (earlier news referenced $31,665 which was a different config). Dimensions cross-checked from dealer site; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch.

changes: Mechanical limited-slip differential and DSC-Track mode
Optional Brembo BBS Recaro performance package

trims.notes: Manual-only trim; Brembo BBS Recaro pkg is an option that adds Brembo front brakes, BBS wheels, Recaro seats.

notable_other: Mazda Radar Cruise Control with Distance & Speed Alert

trims.notes: Base trim of the MX-5 Miata (soft top) 6-speed automatic powertrain line (singleton: sole automatic trim on the soft-top MX-5). Mazda Radar Cruise Control is automatic-only. Curb weight 2,405 lb. Dimensions cross-checked from dealer site; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch.

models.notes: Two powertrain lines on the MX-5 soft top: manual (Sport, Club, Grand Touring 6MT) and automatic (Grand Touring 6AT). Each line has its own base. NHTSA and IIHS do not currently publish ratings for the MX-5 (low-volume convertible).

models.generation_context: Retractable Fastback (RF) hardtop variant of the MX-5 Miata, introduced for 2017 MY. Mazda markets it on its own dedicated mazdausa.com/vehicles/mx-5-miata-rf page. Same 181-hp 2.0L Skyactiv-G; offered in Club and Grand Touring trims only.
models.model_summary: Retractable hardtop variant of the MX-5 Miata, with a power-folding fastback roof that retracts in about 13 seconds. Same 181-hp 2.0L Skyactiv-G as the soft top; offered only in Club (manual-only) and Grand Touring (manual or automatic).

reliability.summary: Shares its powertrain and most mechanicals with the soft-top MX-5; reliability record is similarly strong on Consumer Reports owner surveys.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers like the RF for its hardtop quietness and improved daily usability compared with the soft top, but call out a small weight penalty (~110 lb) and minor cargo penalty from the retractable mechanism.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner aggregates not tracked separately from the soft top.

notable_other: Bilstein dampers and limited-slip diff (Club hardware)

trims.notes: Base trim of the MX-5 Miata RF manual powertrain line. Curb weight ~2,478 lb (about 110 lb more than soft-top Sport). Dimensions cross-checked from dealer site; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch.

notable_other: Brembo BBS Recaro package included standard on RF Club
Brembo front brakes with red calipers
Black Alcantara interior with light gray stitching

changes: Brembo BBS Recaro performance package standard (vs optional on soft top Club)
Brembo front brakes with red calipers

trims.notes: RF Club includes the Brembo BBS Recaro package as standard equipment, distinguishing it from the soft-top Club where it is a separate option.

notable_other: Mazda Radar Cruise Control with Distance & Speed Alert

trims.notes: Base trim of the MX-5 Miata RF automatic powertrain line (singleton). Mazda Radar Cruise Control is automatic-only. Curb weight 2,517 lb. Dimensions cross-checked from dealer site; manufacturer page is the canonical source but was 403 to WebFetch.

models.notes: Two powertrain lines on the RF: manual (Grand Touring 6MT base, Club step-up) and automatic (Grand Touring 6AT singleton). MX-5 Miata RF is catalogued as a separate model per Mazda's dedicated marketing page. NHTSA and IIHS do not publish ratings for the MX-5 line.
